element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns track layouts and car handling without competitive pressure
Player must manage tire hardness before races and plan pitstop strategy
Rapid downshifting and collision consequences create operational constraints
Each difficulty level reduces car durability, increasing collision consequences
Cumulative difficulty across complete season requires sustained performance
Competitors increase speed and aggression alongside player skill demands

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: High-speed continuous tension with resource management relief points (pitstops). Split-screen multiplayer and turbo mode maintain elevated tension throughout. Difficulty progression creates escalating tension curve across championship season.

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Two 'retired' cars visible on Silverstone track serve as environmental markers/obstacles

difficulty_curve.early_game: Novice difficulty with full damage tolerance, slower AI opponents, accessible track learning in training mode
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Amateur and Professional difficulties introduce damage scaling and increased AI speed, tire management becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Expert difficulty combines maximum AI speed with minimum damage tolerance, requires mastery of all mechanical systems
notable_spikes: Transition from Amateur to Professional difficulty increases damage sensitivity significantly
Expert difficulty compounds speed and durability constraints simultaneously
Extended championship seasons accumulate fatigue across 12 consecutive races

notable_level_patterns.description: All 12 tracks include obstacles (signs, adboards, overhead platforms) placed near track edges to increase difficulty. Density and placement vary by track design but serve consistent function of raising challenge level.

All races structured as 7 laps with mandatory pitstop for tire replacement. Pitstop serves as tension-relief boundary dividing race into two phases. Engine overheat risk creates secondary constraint forcing pitstop necessity.

Player can customize championship from 1 to 12 tracks in any order. System allows graduated difficulty: single-track mastery, partial season familiarization, or full season endurance test. Track order matches historical 1993 F1 season when playing full championship.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Championship mode (tracks: Interlagos, Imola, Barcelona, Monte Carlo, Montreal, Castelet, Silverstone, Hockenheim, Spa, Monza, Estoril, Adelaide)

notable_level_patterns.description: Arcade mode uses dynamic scoring system: overtaking actions grant points, running grants points, being overtaken subtracts points. Creates moment-to-moment tension around position changes rather than lap completion.
